title: Loaves, Lamps, and Ledgers
subtitle: Money Wisdom from the King James Bible for Real Life
author: Shandy Lindley

chapters.title: Foreword
chapters.content:
FOREWORD

Money reveals what we trust.

Some people trust their paycheck.
Some trust their credit limit.
Some trust their hustle.
Some trust what others think.

But stewardship is different.
Stewardship says, "This is not mine to worship. This is mine to manage."

The King James Bible does not treat money like a toy.
It treats money like a test.

This book is not built to impress.
It is built to steady.
It is built to strengthen.
It is built to help a household breathe again.

"The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it." (Proverbs 10:22 KJV)

chapters.title: Introduction: Why Stewardship Matters
chapters.content:
INTRODUCTION

Financial stress is rarely mathematical alone.
It is spiritual, emotional, and behavioral.

You can earn more and still drown.
You can earn less and still build.
The difference is stewardship.

Stewardship is not ownership.
It is management under God.

"The earth is the LORD'S, and the fulness thereof." (Psalm 24:1 KJV)

This book moves from alignment to legacy.
Not hype.
Not pressure.
Not shame.

Alignment first.
Then clarity.
Then consistency.
Then peace.

Read slowly.
Apply steadily.
Build faithfully.

chapters.title: The Hands That Hold the Loaves
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 1
The Hands That Hold the Loaves

Provision is rarely lightning.
Most of the time it is bread in the hands of faithful people.

"In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread." (Genesis 3:19 KJV)

TEACHING
Work is not punishment when it is done with purpose.
Work becomes worship when it is honest, steady, and accountable.

Diligence is not glamour.
Diligence is governance.
It guards your future one ordinary day at a time.

"Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings." (Proverbs 22:29 KJV)

STORY
A father wanted a miracle month.
He wanted bills to vanish.
But God kept pointing him back to one thing.

One more quote.
One more call.
One more shift.
One more evening of disciplined effort.

He did not become wealthy overnight.
He became stable.
And stability became peace.

"The hand of the diligent maketh rich." (Proverbs 10:4 KJV)

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Write down your income and your fixed bills.
2) Pick one simple habit that increases diligence.
3) Treat your schedule like stewardship.

PRAYER
Lord, steady my hands and sanctify my work. Teach me diligence without pride and provision without fear. Amen.

chapters.title: The Lamp and the Ledger
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 2
The Lamp and the Ledger

A budget is not bondage.
It is a lantern.
A ledger is not shame.
It is clarity.

"Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path." (Psalm 119:105 KJV)

TEACHING
Many people do not have a money problem.
They have a visibility problem.

What you do not track will quietly drain you.
What you do track will start to obey.

"For which of you, intending to build a tower, sitteth not down first, and counteth the cost." (Luke 14:28 KJV)

STORY
A woman avoided her bank app like a bad doctor report.
One night she opened it and wrote everything down.
It hurt for ten minutes.
Then it healed for ten months.

Clarity became courage.
Courage became action.

"In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips tendeth only to penury." (Proverbs 14:23 KJV)

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Track every dollar for 7 days.
2) Label each expense.
3) Keep what feeds your mission. Cut what feeds your mood.

PRAYER
Lord, light my path. Teach me honest numbers and holy discipline. Make my planning wise and my heart calm. Amen.

chapters.title: The Firstfruits and the Field
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 3
The Firstfruits and the Field

A field yields when seed meets order.

"Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase." (Proverbs 3:9 KJV)

TEACHING
Firstfruits is not about what God needs.
It is about what you need.

First positions your heart before your hands ever touch the rest.
It breaks the lie that money is your master.

"But seek ye first the kingdom of God, and his righteousness; and all these things shall be added unto you." (Matthew 6:33 KJV)

STORY
A family waited to give until they felt safe.
Safe never came.
So they flipped the order.

First they honored God.
Then they tracked the rest.
Peace arrived before extra money did.

"Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap." (Galatians 6:7 KJV)

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Choose a firstfruits number you can be faithful to.
2) Give it first.
3) Then budget the rest with clarity.

PRAYER
Lord, steady my hands and set my heart in order. Help me honor you first and steward the rest with wisdom. Amen.

chapters.title: Bread, Burden, and the Building of Margin
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 4
Bread, Burden, and the Building of Margin

Money is not only about what you earn.
It is about what you carry.
Some carry bread.
Some carry burden.

"The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender." (Proverbs 22:7 KJV)

TEACHING
Debt is weight.
It narrows choices.
It reduces margin.

Margin is space.
Breathing room between income and expense.
It is the quiet strength of living below your means.

"Owe no man any thing, but to love one another." (Romans 13:8 KJV)

DILIGENCE
"He becometh poor that dealeth with a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich." (Proverbs 10:4 KJV)

STORY
A man prayed for increase while ignoring habits.
He worked when convenient.
Spent when emotional.
Borrowed when pressured.

Then he tracked every dollar.
Cut what was unnecessary.
Got consistent.
Progress was slow, then peace showed up.

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) List every debt clearly.
2) Create margin by cutting one unnecessary expense.
3) Apply extra money toward one debt consistently.

PRAYER
Lord, give me discipline in my labor, wisdom in my spending, and courage to remove burdens from my house. Amen.

chapters.title: The Ant and the Emergency Fund
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 5
The Ant and the Emergency Fund

Crisis does not schedule itself.
It arrives.
Wisdom prepares.

"Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise." (Proverbs 6:6 KJV)

TEACHING
Savings is not greed.
It is preparedness.

An emergency fund is not a flex.
It is a shield.

"A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself." (Proverbs 22:3 KJV)

STORY
A couple lived paycheck to paycheck until one tire blew.
Then the next month a medical bill landed.

They started saving twenty dollars a week.
It felt small.
Then the next tire blew and it did not become a disaster.
That is what a shield looks like.

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Start with a small target: $500.
2) Automate a weekly transfer.
3) Only use it for true emergencies.

PRAYER
Lord, teach me preparation without fear. Help me build a quiet shield for my household. Amen.

chapters.title: Contentment, Comparison, and Clean Spending
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 6
Contentment, Comparison, and Clean Spending

Comparison is a thief.
It steals peace, then calls it ambition.

"But godliness with contentment is great gain." (1 Timothy 6:6 KJV)

TEACHING
Spending is often emotional.
The cart fills up when the heart is empty.

Contentment is not settling.
It is strength.
It is the power to say, "Not today," and mean it.

"Having food and raiment let us be therewith content." (1 Timothy 6:8 KJV)

STORY
A man upgraded everything but his peace.
He finally wrote down what he was trying to prove.
When the need for applause died, the budget lived.

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Unfollow what fuels envy.
2) Wait 24 hours before non essential purchases.
3) Buy for function, not for applause.

PRAYER
Lord, cleanse my motives. Teach me contentment that does not shrink my faith, only my noise. Amen.

chapters.title: Stewardship in the House
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 7
Stewardship in the House

A budget that divides a marriage is not wisdom.
Stewardship must be shared.

"Can two walk together, except they be agreed?" (Amos 3:3 KJV)

TEACHING
Money becomes dangerous when it becomes secret.
Unity beats income.

Your household does not need a perfect spreadsheet.
It needs an honest table.

"Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation." (Matthew 12:25 KJV)

STORY
A couple fought monthly, then they started holding a short meeting every week.
Fifteen minutes.
Numbers, plans, prayer.
Less drama.
More direction.

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Weekly money meeting: 15 minutes.
2) One shared goal.
3) One shared sacrifice.

PRAYER
Lord, make us one in purpose. Give us clarity without conflict and discipline without pride. Amen.

chapters.title: Generosity That Does Not Break the Home
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 8
Generosity That Does Not Break the Home

Giving is worship, not performance.
But generosity must be stewarded.

"It is more blessed to give than to receive." (Acts 20:35 KJV)

TEACHING
God loves cheerful giving, not pressured giving.
Generosity should not create chaos in the house.
It should create joy and order.

"Every man according as he purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not grudgingly, or of necessity." (2 Corinthians 9:7 KJV)

STORY
A man gave big once, then panicked for weeks.
Later he set a steady plan.
Smaller.
Consistent.
Faithful.
Joy replaced anxiety.

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Choose a giving plan that is sustainable.
2) Give consistently.
3) Leave room for unexpected compassion.

PRAYER
Lord, make my hands open and my house stable. Teach me cheerful giving with wise boundaries. Amen.

chapters.title: Legacy, Inheritance, and Long Obedience
chapters.content:
CHAPTER 9
Legacy, Inheritance, and Long Obedience

Legacy is not a quote on a wall.
Legacy is habits that outlive you.

"A good man leaveth an inheritance to his children's children." (Proverbs 13:22 KJV)

TEACHING
Inheritance is not only money.
It is training.

A child who learns stewardship receives a treasure even if the number is small.

"Train up a child in the way he should go." (Proverbs 22:6 KJV)

STORY
A father taught his kids to budget ten dollars.
Not to shame them, but to shape them.

Years later, the kids were calm adults.
Because calm is taught.

PRACTICAL STEPS
1) Write down what your family stands for.
2) Teach money basics in small bites.
3) Put your plans in writing.

PRAYER
Lord, help me live long obedience. Let my household inherit wisdom, not just stuff. Amen.

chapters.title: Benediction
chapters.content:
BENEDICTION

May your work be diligent.
May your spending be wise.
May your giving be joyful.
May your legacy be enduring.

"Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think." (Ephesians 3:20 KJV)

Walk faithfully.
Build steadily.
Leave wisely.
